reflection:
Yesterday hit you with brain fog and that dragging sleepiness, and you wrote it down two days running now. Vision blurring, slow thinking, almost dizzy at times. That is not nothing. Worth flagging.

Your body did its part though. Borough Market down south bank to Tower Hill, ten thousand steps, ate properly at ARABICA. Mind just would not catch up.

You also nailed the Telfast link with the eczema on your thumb. Good clinical instinct, you halved the dose and the skin reacted within a day. Tell whoever is steering the antihistamine call so it does not sit as an open loop.

The thing to call out: two evenings now you wrote up on computer too long and Clauding around, and two mornings you woke up groggy. Late screens plus heavy cognitive lift from building that email pipeline are stacking on a body that is still recovering from the intensive London treatment week. You finished the PM routine last night, screens off, breathwork, lights, all done. Good. But the laptop kept running past it.

One thing for today: hard cut on the laptop by 21:30, no exceptions. The Claude project will keep. Your eyes and head need the gap.

The quiet good news underneath the fog: sciatica gone, coccyx calmer at 3, anxiety at 1, no acid, no throat tightness. You are moving in the right direction. Just give the brain some recovery room today.
opener: fog and sleepiness back to back, but the body is doing its part
focus_for_today: hard laptop cut by 21:30, no exceptions
